channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇫🇷🇷🇺 French jets intercept Russian aircraft 11 times over the Baltics in one week
item.description: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times as part of NATO Baltic Air Policing Mission, with Frances armed forces spokesperson describing higher-than-usual Russian provocations amid ongoing Ukraine war tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇪🇺 Sweden urges EU to tighten tourist visa rules for Russians
item.description: Swedens migration minister called for stricter EU rules on Russian tourist visas, saying it was insane that many Russians can still travel freely despite the ongoing war in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon ceasefire terms, calls deal an illusion
item.description: Hezbollah leader Sheikh Naim Qassam rejected the ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon, saying terms requiring Hezbollah to stop firing and withdraw from the south would mean surrender and defeat.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran foreign minister says no tangible progress in US talks
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said messages are still being exchanged with the US but no formal negotiations are underway, warning that any Israeli attack on Beirut would trigger full-scale resumption of war.

item.title: 🇺🇸💼 US will uphold tariff caps in trade deals with EU and Japan, trade chief says
item.description: US Trade Representative confirmed the US will respect tariff caps in trade agreements struck with the European Union, Japan and other countries, amid ongoing trade negotiations and tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel and Lebanon agree to renew ceasefire with security zones excluding Hezbollah
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to renew their fragile ceasefire and create pilot security zones inside Lebanon from which Hezbollah would be banned, contingent on complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and evacuation of operatives from areas south of the Litani River.

item.title: 🇵🇸💔 11 killed in Gaza strikes in 24 hours as death toll since cease-fire nears 1,000
item.description: Palestinian hospitals report 11 people killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza overnight, bringing the death toll since the ceasefire began in January to nearly 1,000 people.

item.title: 🇯🇵💹 Bank of Japan expected to raise interest rates in June
item.description: The Bank of Japan is expected to raise interest rates at its upcoming June meeting as the central bank continues to normalize monetary policy amid inflationary pressures.

item.title: 🇸🇪⚖️ Sweden seizes vessel suspected of illegally exporting Ukrainian grain from Russian-occupied territories
item.description: Swedish authorities have seized a vessel suspected of illegally transporting Ukrainian grain stolen from Russian-occupied territories, part of ongoing efforts to crack down on the illicit trade.

item.title: 🇮🇩🏛️ Indonesia arrests officials in anti-corruption crackdown
item.description: Indonesian authorities have arrested multiple officials as part of a major anti-corruption crackdown targeting graft across multiple government agencies.

item.title: 🇳🇵🏔️ Nepali Mount Everest guide found alive after six days missing
item.description: A Nepali mountain guide who went missing on Mount Everest six days ago has been found alive, in a remarkable survival story on the worlds highest peak.

item.title: 🇩🇪🏛️ Ex-Merkel advisor links German support for Israel to failed UN Security Council bid
item.description: A former advisor to Chancellor Angela Merkel said Germanys strong support for Israel was connected to Berlins failed attempt to secure a UN Security Council seat, revealing diplomatic calculations behind the policy.

item.title: 🇪🇺🌍 EU Council chief calls Western Balkans enlargement most important geopolitical investment
item.description: European Council President Antonio Costa called EU enlargement to the Western Balkans the most important geopolitical investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the region for European security.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇭🇺 Ukraine-Hungary minority rights agreement clears path for EU accession
item.description: Ukraine and Hungary reached an agreement on minority rights that clears the path for Ukraines EU accession process, removing a key obstacle that Hungary had used to block progress.

item.title: 🇬🇱🇩🇰 Rubio says Greenland is part of Denmark for now
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio acknowledged that Greenland is part of Denmark for now, while making clear that Washington continues to have interests in the strategic Arctic territory.

item.title: 🇧🇳👑 Bruneis Sultan appoints sons as ministers in cabinet shake-up
item.description: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah announced a major cabinet reshuffle, appointing his sons to ministerial positions in a significant restructuring of Bruneis government.

item.title: 🇨🇳🕵️ China bans four New Zealand lawmakers after Taiwan visit
item.description: China imposed one-year travel bans on four New Zealand lawmakers who visited Taiwan, marking the first time Beijing has sanctioned New Zealand parliamentarians over Taiwan visits.

item.title: 🇩🇪🗳️ Germany puts brave face on UN Security Council defeat
item.description: Germany addressed its defeat in the UN Security Council election, with officials emphasizing continued commitment to multilateralism despite losing the vote to Greece.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚖️ US House backs resolution curbing Trump Iran war powers
item.description: The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to limit President Trumps ability to continue the war with Iran without congressional authorization, marking the first time the lower chamber has defied the White House on the conflict.

item.title: 🇷🇺✈️ Russia stumbles in air war strategy despite battlefield claims
item.description: Despite claims of air superiority, Russia continues to struggle with its air war strategy in Ukraine, revealing gaps between military rhetoric and operational reality.

item.title: 🇬🇧📰 UK appalled by Russia ban on several journalists
item.description: The United Kingdom expressed strong condemnation after Russia banned several British journalists from entering the country, calling it another attempt to suppress independent reporting.

item.title: 🇷🇺🤝 Taliban and Russia cozying up to each other
item.description: Russia and the Taliban are strengthening ties as Moscow seeks regional allies and Kabul looks for international legitimacy, marking a significant shift in geopolitical alignments.

item.title: 🇬🇧🕵️ China targeted British parliamentarians in spying operations, report reveals
item.description: British intelligence revealed that China targeted multiple UK parliamentarians in coordinated spying operations, raising concerns about foreign influence campaigns in democratic institutions.

item.title: 🇸🇦🛡️ Saudis wartime resilience tested as Kuwait and Bahrain face Iranian attacks
item.description: Overnight attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain have delivered a sharp jolt to a fragile ceasefire, with Iran striking Kuwaits airport and Bahrain intercepting missiles targeting US military positions.

item.title: 🇮🇷⛽ Oil shipments continue escaping Hormuz as traders adapt to war risks
item.description: The trickle of tankers exiting the Strait of Hormuz has gathered pace as traders adopt stealth measures, previewing the fragmented energy market the Iran war is creating.
